checks.a_analysis: The output text strictly follows the content of the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or headings were found. All headings and body text elements correspond directly to the text visible in the resolution image.
The document contains no contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs) other than names of officials. No fabricated contact details were added in the conversion.

The single-page resolution is fully represented. All sections including the location header, title, 'WHEREAS' clauses, 'NOW THEREFORE' clause, date line, and signature lines (Mark F. Richardson and Susan O. Washburn) are present in the output.
There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant text was omitted. The transcription of the clauses and the resolution statement is complete and accurate to the source image.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the JSON output is a faithful transcription of the source image. Wording, names, and dates (March 6, 2023) match the original document perfectly. Whitespace and line breaks are handled appropriately for a digital conversion.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the source PDF and the provided JSON output. Each section of text from the source, including the header, the resolution title, the 'WHEREAS' clauses, the resolution statement, the date, and the signature blocks, appears exactly once in the JSON. There is no evidence of duplicated content.

The JSON output contains a single heading (level 2) which corresponds to the main title of the resolution. Using an H2 for the document title is acceptable under the normalization guidelines for accessibility. There are no other headings, so there are no hierarchy skips or nesting issues.
The source document does not contain any tables. Therefore, this check is not applicable and passes.

The JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom reading order of the document: Header -> Title -> Body -> Signatures -> Seal. Although the physical seal is located to the right of the clerk signature in the original layout, placing it at the end of the sequence in the JSON is standard for linearized, accessible reading order and does not represent a structural defect.
The source document does not contain any bulleted or numbered lists. The series of 'WHEREAS' clauses are correctly identified as individual paragraphs.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The JSON contains 1 heading and the HTML contains 2. The mismatch of 1 heading is less than the threshold of 3 required to fail this check.

The JSON contains 0 links and the HTML contains 1. As there are no links in the source document, this single HTML link likely represents an accessibility skip-link or a pipeline-added footer link, both of which are permitted. There is no evidence of incorrect or fabricated content-bearing links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML maintains the logical flow and single-column organization of the source document. The placement of the seal at the bottom instead of to the right of the signature block is an expected adjustment for a responsive HTML layout compared to the fixed PDF layout. The general sequence of information (State/County, Title, Whereas clauses, Resolution, Date, Signatures) is preserved.
The source document does not contain any tables.

The source PDF contains two handwritten signatures (for Mark F. Richardson and Susan O. Washburn) which are essential visual components of a signed resolution. While the text names are present, the graphical signatures themselves are missing from the HTML output. The official seal is present.
checks.b_evidence_source: Handwritten signatures appearing above the lines for 'MARK F. RICHARDSON, CHAIRMAN' and 'SUSAN O. WASHBURN'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML provides the text names but omits the images of the actual signatures.
checks.b_evidence_location: Signature block at the bottom of the page.

checks.a_analysis: The main resolution title is visually duplicated in the HTML output. It appears first inside the <header> tag as an <h1> element and immediately again inside the <main> section as an <h2> element. The source document only displays this title once.
checks.b_evidence_source: RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF ROCKINGHAM COUNTY 911 EMERGENCY TELECOMMUNICATORS

checks.a_analysis: The overall visual quality is slightly degraded due to the omission of the signature lines (horizontal rules) that define the signing block in the original document. This, combined with the missing signatures and duplicated title, reduces the formal polish expected of a government resolution.
checks.b_evidence_source: Horizontal lines placed above the Chairman's and Clerk's printed names.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text is present without the supporting horizontal lines used for anchoring signatures.
checks.b_evidence_location: Signature block at the bottom of the page.
